channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Wed, 15 Jul 2026 02:55:48 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Indian man Anshul Kuncha's murder in Philadelphia: 14-year-old boy arrested, charged
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Plutarch: 'I don't need a friend who changes when I change and who nods when I nod; my shadow does that much better' and the true meaning of friendship
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Asmongold ends years of roach memes after revealing his house is finally bug-free
item.description: Twitch streamer Asmongold reports his house is now nearly bug-free after renovations. He has not seen a cockroach in his home for nearly one year. Major upgrades like new siding and renovations have made his house more secure. Asmongold plans to hire professional cleaners to address remaining mess soon. This update follows years of jokes about his infamous messy living conditions.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Kick streamer SuzieSmalls dies after being found dead in her apartment
item.description: Kick streamer SuzieSmalls, whose real name was Suzette, was found deceased. Her channel moderator confirmed the tragic news on the morning of July thirteenth. Suzie gained popularity through her IRL streams and Fishtank Live appearances. Fans and fellow creators are now sharing tributes and expressing their grief. The cause of her death has not yet been officially announced.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Quote of the day by Heraclitus: 'No man ever steps in the same river twice, for it's not the same river and he's not the same man' and a lesson on how change is the only constant
item.description: Quote of the day: Ancient Greek thinker Heraclitus asserted that reality is a ceaseless flow, with fire symbolizing the unending process of transformation. His river metaphor articulates that stability is an illusion, as even our sense of self is transient.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meet Alex Sunny, American woman going viral on TikTok for her intention to relocate to Afghanistan for husband she met on Snapchat
item.description: Alex Sunny, a viral TikTok user , plans to move to Afghanistan permanently this August. She met her husband online and they married after a brief in-person meeting. Sunny claims since her husband can't travel to the US because of the travel ban, she will be relocating to Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Warren Buffett stops yearly donation to Gates Foundation over Epstein link: 'Of course, morality is unpredicted but my remaining shares will be...'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: E Jean Carroll receives $5.6 million in sexual abuse case after years-long legal battle with Trump
item.description: Writer E. Jean Carroll has received over $5.6 million in damages awarded in her civil case against US President Donald Trump after a federal court released funds held in escrow during the appeals process. The payment follows the US Supreme Court's decision to let the 2023 verdict stand. Trump continues to challenge the ruling and has filed further appeals.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian-origin Florida man targeted in a fake 'Muslim city' conspiracy; he files lawsuit, says he's not even Muslim
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: What happened to JiDion after his viral Kick arrest? Court update brings an unexpected twist
item.description: JiDion says his legal case is officially over after all charges from his June 22 arrest were dismissed with prejudice following a July 13 court hearing. He said everyone arrested with him also had their records expunged. After the case ended, the streamer repeated that he is leaving livestream prank content behind and will now focus on predator-catching videos.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Several dead in Brussels construction site fire, say authorities
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: UK wasted £9.9 billion on Covid PPE, finds inquiry; stockpile, procurement flaws flagged
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian man dies in skydiving accident in Massachusetts, fell from 30 feet above in 'sudden, unexpected gust of wind'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: After renting in Dubai for 15 years, is buying now finally cheaper? The five numbers every tenant should calculate
item.description: Many Dubai tenants question buying property after years of rent payments. Experts advise focusing on current finances and future plans for this decision. Owning a home offers stability and equity while protecting against rising rental costs. Buyers should carefully calculate costs and consider their long-term commitment to Dubai. Focusing on personal circumstances is more crucial than predicting market shifts.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: A Labrador began swaying and could no longer walk on Britain’s highest mountain; rescuers later discovered what she had eaten
item.description: A black Labrador named Tokyo collapsed during a hike up Ben Nevis in Scotland. Vets suspected the dog ingested discarded cannabis which caused her serious illness. The Lochaber Mountain Rescue Team assisted in bringing the unwell dog down the mountain. Tokyo received treatment at a veterinary clinic and made a full recovery. Her owner now warns other pet owners about potential discarded drugs on trails.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Watch video: 'Native' Canadian woman blames Indians for throwing trash during Canada Day celebrations, says 'you should be ashamed'
item.description: A woman confronted volunteers at a Calgary park over littering during Canada Day celebrations. She expressed her disappointment with the scattered garbage across the public space. Volunteers from the Guru Nanak Free Kitchen and Ahmadiyya Muslim Community stayed behind to clean. They clarified that the food service items were not from their stalls. The organizers later shared photos showing the park completely cleaned after the event.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meet Mira Trivedi, Indian-origin student in New Jersey who's pushing her school to not call Hitler's symbol 'swastika'
item.description: In New Jersey, student Mira Trivedi is on a mission to clarify the misunderstanding surrounding the swastika, often mistakenly associated with Nazi imagery. Advocating for its roots in Hindu and Buddhist traditions, she initially faced obstacles but has since garnered support from classmates and parents alike.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Britain bought 1,525 acres nearly lifeless farmland, now beavers are helping it make money
item.description: A farm in Lincolnshire is evolving into a lucrative wildland endeavor, with beavers playing a pivotal role in this ecological project. The initiative focuses on demonstrating that restoring nature can lead to substantial private income. Current revenue streams are generated through carbon removal and biodiversity credits, with aspirations to draw significant investments for sustainable environmental solutions.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meet Carol Ruckdeschel: 84-year-old 'wildest woman in America' who has been living on an island for the 53 years in order to preserve nature for the future
item.description: Carol Ruckdeschel has lived on Cumberland Island for 53 years, preserving its natural state. She meticulously documents the island's diverse flora and fauna in her field journals. Her dedication led to legislative changes protecting sea turtles from shrimping trawlers. Ruckdeschel sustains herself by living off the land and off the grid. She continues to advocate for the island's preservation against development proposals.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Two brothers detained by Pakistani forces found dead in Balochistan; custodial death concerns raised
item.description: Two brothers, Imam and Muhammad Umar, were found dead with bullet injuries in Balochistan. They were allegedly taken into custody by security personnel after their motorcycle ran out of fuel. Their family later received their bodies, disputing claims of militant links. Local residents expressed concern over the circumstances surrounding their deaths. No official response has been issued by Pakistan's military or government.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
